top_signals.title: Kuwait Bond Sale Bids Top $9 Billion Even as Iran Strikes
top_signals.summary: Kuwait has drawn more than $9 billion of investor orders for a dollar bond sale on Wednesday, highlighting resilient demand for the OPEC member’s debt even as it comes under daily missile and drone attacks from Iran.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Microsoft to stop Exchange 2016 / 2019 security updates in October
top_signals.summary: Microsoft has reminded customers that it will stop shipping security updates for Exchange 2016 and 2019 through the Extended Security Update (ESU) program in October. [...]

top_signals.title: Testing times require time for testing − speech by Ruth Smith
Personal Income and Outlays, April 2026
top_signals.summary: Personal income decreased less than $0.1 billion (less than 0.1 percent at a monthly rate) in April, according to estimates released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA). Disposable personal income (DPI)-personal income less personal current taxes-decreased $19.9 billion (0.1 percent), and personal consumption expenditures (PCE) increased $111.1 billion (0.5 percent). Personal outlays-the sum of PCE, personal interest payments, and personal current transfer payments-increased $114.0 billion in April. Personal saving was $611.7 billion in April, and the personal saving rate-personal saving as a percentage of DPI-was 2.6 percent. Full Text

top_signals.title: Prologis tables ‘best and final’ £14bn offer for Segro
top_signals.summary: US real estate firm Prologis has tabled a “best and final” £14bn offer for Segro, the FTSE 100 property company it has been pursuing for weeks.  The Californian property company said its latest offer, which values Segro at £10.32 per share, is a “compelling opportunity for both sets of shareholders”.  This bid marks the latest [...]
